'''Morcanth''' was an [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighborhood of which was the subject of a [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]-long [[Unidentified war (Morcanth)|war]] between three [[Spacefaring/Legends|spacefaring]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. At one point, the corrupted [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Winslau Da'k]] [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] his former [[Winslau Da'k's Jedi Master|Jedi Master]] at a [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] on Morcanth, where Da'k resided in wealth and comfort. After the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] sought out help from a group of agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the rebels ultimately orchestrated an assault on Da'k's fortress and successfully rescued the Jedi.

==Description==
Morcanth was an obscure terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TLK" /> located in the [[Morcanth system/Legends|Morcanth system]] of the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
[[File:UnknownRegions-TEA.png|thumb|right|200px|Morcanth was located in the Unknown Regions.]]
Morcanth was situated in an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] over which, by the time of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], three powerful [[Spacefaring/Legends|spacefaring]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] had been waging a [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]-long [[Unidentified war (Morcanth)|war]]. At some point after the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the Empire<ref name="TLK" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] student [[Winslau Da'k]], who had become corrupted by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], attacked and overpowered his [[Winslau Da'k's Jedi Master|Jedi Master]]. Da'k then [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] the older Jedi at a hidden [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] on Morcanth, where he subjected him to [[Torture/Legends|torture]]. At one point, the planet's name was changed, with its original name subsequently only appearing in very old [[Star map/Legends|star charts]].<ref name="TLK" />

{{RPG|"[[The Last Knight]]"}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Da'k's Jedi Master reached out [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] to a group of Alliance agents, pleading with them to rescue him. The Rebels knew the Jedi was [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] on a planet called Morcanth but were initially unable to find it on any [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Map/Legends|charts]]. However, they eventually discovered the world and, making peace with one of the species warring over the planet's cosmic environs, recruited the aliens to launch an assault on Da'k's fortress while they mounted a successful rescue of the fallen Jedi's Master.<ref name="TLK" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
At the fortress on Morcanth, where he lived in wealth and comfort, Winslau Da'k was accompanied by a small retinue of servants and guards. Da'k's small private [[Army/Legends|army]] included advanced [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="TLK" />

==Locations==
Morcanth was the site of a hidden underground fortress, the defenses of which included heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] emplacements.<ref name="TLK" />

==Behind the scenes==
Morcanth is the setting for "[[The Last Knight]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure hook written by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] published in the [[1992]] supplement ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'' for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. "The Last Knight" suggests an optional modification of its events where Winslau Da'k employs a more significant military force, thereby forcing the player characters to seek aid from at least one of the alien species engaged in conflict over Morcanth and its [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighborhood. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="TLK" />
